summary: Joel describes how the Copilot team broke out of a months-long rut where they were obsessively chasing an obscure bug, inspired by a Seth Godin post about focusing intensely to finish projects. A student in their management training program rallied the developers to adopt a 'hair on fire' focus strategy with daily standup meetings. In about six weeks they went from stuck to shipping Copilot OneClick, a new feature for persistent remote desktop connections. The post serves as both a product announcement and a case study in how motivational focus can unstick a stalled team.
key_points: The Copilot team had spent months stuck in an obsessive hunt for an obscure edge-case bug, losing sight of shipping
A Seth Godin post about intense focus ('like your hair is on fire') inspired the team to change their approach and institute daily standup meetings
In six weeks (three to code complete, three for testing and polish) they shipped Copilot OneClick, a significant usability feature
Copilot OneClick lets users install persistent connections to frequently-accessed computers, extending the product beyond ad-hoc tech support
money_quotes: The Copilot team had spent a couple of months stuck in a bizarre Moby Dick-style obsessive hunt to fix a very obscure bug in a very rare edge case in some code which nobody would ever see.
There was a loooong period of time there where every once in a while I would ask Ben what was going on and he would say, 'we should have AutoUpdate done today.'
The eleventh time I heard that it was going to be done 'today' I started to detect a pattern.

In their weakened state from an exhausting chase after one very annoying bug, they probably could have been convinced that it was a good idea to try hang gliding from the roof of our office building to the Statue of Liberty.
It took about three weeks to get to code complete and about three more weeks of testing and polishing, but lo and behold, it's here: Copilot OneClick!
key_insight: Teams can break out of months-long ruts by deliberately refocusing with intense urgency and daily accountability, turning stalled projects into shipped products in weeks.
